display.purpose: Hero moments or major screen titles.

headline.purpose: Primary page headers and prominent section dividers.

title.purpose: Card titles, folder names, or list item headers.

shadows.name: ambient-glow
shadows.purpose: Soft, tinted shadow for cards in Light Mode.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #012D1D; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 16px 24px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; border: none; border-radius: 24px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1B4332; }

components.name: Standard Card
components.description: Rounded container for content and lists.
components.html:
Document Title

This is the body content of the card, using Inter for high legibility.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FAF9F4; padding: 16px; border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(27, 67, 50, 0.08); } .ds-card-title { font-family: Lora, serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; color: #1B1C19; margin: 0 0 8px 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; color: #414844; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: The design system for Quire evokes the quiet focus of a well-lit library and the tactile satisfaction of a curated study desk. The personality is disciplined yet inviting—clean, warm, and highly organized. The aesthetic leans into Modern Corporate with Minimalist influences, utilizing high-quality serif typography for content to provide a scholarly rhythm. The interface prioritizes focus by reducing visual noise, using deep greens and warm ambers to create a grounded, studious atmosphere that differentiates itself from typical cold, clinical productivity tools.
keyCharacteristics: Warm off-white backgrounds (Light Mode) and deep charcoal (Dark Mode) to reduce eye strain
Soft, pill-shaped buttons and generously rounded cards

rules.name: The Warmth Rule
rules.body: Neutral surfaces in Light Mode should never be pure white (#FFFFFF) unless used as an absolute highlight. They must always carry a slight warm tint (#FAF9F4) to feel tactile and printed.

rules.name: The Scholarly Rhythm Rule
rules.body: Headings must use Lora to establish the academic context, while all functional UI text (buttons, metadata, navigation) strictly uses Inter to maintain instant legibility.

rules.name: The Tonal Depth Rule
rules.body: In Dark Mode, depth is achieved by lightening the surface color (e.g., #161716 or #2A2B2A) against the deeper background (#0F100F), rather than using drop shadows.

dos: Do use extremely soft, tinted shadows (e.g., rgba(27, 67, 50, 0.08)) instead of generic gray/black drop shadows.
Do maintain a 1.5x line height for body text to ensure readability during heavy research/reading tasks.
Do ensure all vertical spacing is a multiple of the 8px base unit.
donts: Don't use pure white (#FFFFFF) as a main background in Light Mode; always use the Warm Off-White (#FAF9F4).
Don't mix Lora and Inter within the same paragraph or block. Use Lora for the header, Inter for the body.
Don't use identical, endless card grids without visual breaks.
